commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The blocker is a concrete, narrow repair to preserve the valid GIF classification while moving metadata to the existing untrusted context path.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a prompt-boundary concern by serializing untrusted WhatsApp provider metadata into the agent-facing message body.; Review finding: [P1] Route external ad metadata through untrusted context — extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract... (sha=a6e58be04b85a5a9802527325c86ea8c2ade802f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WhatsApp inbound media placeholder extraction so GIF videos can emit `<media:gif>` and image/video placeholders can include `externalAdReply` title, source, and description attributes.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +83. Total +111 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still maps WhatsApp video messages to `<media:video>` and does not read `gifPlayback` or `externalAdReply`; I did not exercise a live WhatsApp GIF-picker payload in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a concrete, narrow repair to preserve the valid GIF classification while moving metadata to the existing untrusted context path.
